description: Relying on a push notification as the only trigger for important state, which silently breaks when delivery is dropped or delayed.
body: Failure mode: a feature updates critical state only when a push arrives, so when APNs/FCM throttles, collapses, or drops the message the state is never updated and the user sees stale or missing data. Detection signals: events go missing when the device was offline; behavior differs by network conditions; no foreground reconciliation path. Do not treat push delivery as guaranteed for critical flows. Safer fallback: reconcile state with a server fetch on foreground, make push handlers idempotent, handle token rotation, and verify correctness when a push is intentionally dropped.
